name: Corporate disputes
window_name: Arbitration corporate disputes — litigation by MRZ LEGAL.
meta_description: We handle corporate disputes. We resolve complex corporate conflicts related to the participation and management of a legal entity, transactions with shares in the capital of companies.

blocks.text_main:
Engaging in business, whether under your own name or someone else’s, using your personal investment, funds from banks, or partners can be a contentious matter when you are not the sole proprietor.

Good decisions are made when the interests of a company’s directors, shareholders, and controlling persons align towards the shared economic goal of profit-making from the business.

One day, this goal may no longer unify them, and at that point, a manager will begin to treat the assets of a legal entity as his own, while the individual shareholder will perceive themselves as the sole owner. This will inevitably result in financial losses, a decrease in equity, or withdrawal from the market.

No matter the ownership percentage and scale of the company, individuals at any time can become embroiled in a corporate conflict. This can happen when certain partners aim to eliminate others or when competitors display their interest. Furthermore, these risks become more important as the entity’s assets expand in both size and value.

One of the potential risks includes the possibility of seizing the entity’s assets via consideration that lacks value, usually in the form of its own securities. These assets would then be transferred through a sequence of transactions to a buyer, that is seemingly “bona fide”, or alienated based on fictitious grounds.

At times, such conflicts do not involve any intention to cause harm to the entity or those who benefit. For example, when a bank enforces corporate control to recover loans.

The number of hostile takeovers and corporate raiders is increasing, leading to new cases in corporate disputes, where we successfully defend our clients’ business decisions and assets.
